method: Two independent research passes (2026-07-18) merged with conflict adjudication at primary sources; every field carries sources or is null with a reason. Nulls are deliberate: a null beats a guess.

records.settingName: Field of View (built-in Field of View Calculator)

convention.basis: full rendered span; in native three-projection mode this is the total horizontal span across all three projections
convention.note: Official docs establish the geometry-driven calculator, native three-projection rendering, and the 270-degree triple limit; explicit horizontal-axis NAMING comes from converging secondary sources, not a first-party sentence.

range.conditions: 270 limit officially documented for triple driving cameras; 45 minimum secondary-verified; single-screen max not independently verified

records.menuPath: Settings > Display > Monitor > Field of View Calculator; in-sim [ / ] hotkeys
records.configFile: Documents\iRacing\app.ini (view); Documents\iRacing\rendererDX11Monitor.ini (monitor geometry only — holds NO FOV-degrees key)
records.tripleNotes: Select 3 Flat/Curved Screens, enter width, bezel, viewing distance, side angle; enable 'Render scene using 3 projections' (RenderViewPerMonitor=1). Map the calculator's FULL visible horizontal envelope to the driving FOV — never the center-panel span.
records.notes: Both research engines agree on geometry/triple model; they split on axis-claim strength (community-only vs official-implied), so confidence is held at medium until a first-party axis sentence is found. Community material reports an app.ini key named drivingCamFOV, but no direct supporting source was verified; configKey is therefore null. step null: undocumented.

convention.basis: single-screen value, independent of aspect ratio
convention.note: Convention from converging secondary sources; no surviving first-party statement found.

records.menuPath: On track > right-side app bar > Onboard Settings > FOV
records.tripleNotes: Native Triple Screen rendering mode with in-race app: per-screen Rotation/Angle, Distance, bezel Margins. In triple mode the geometry supersedes the scalar FOV — enter physical measurements; use spans only for validation.
records.notes: Range/config-file claims come from secondary config-reference sources. Community material reports a [MODE].FOV key, but no direct supporting source was verified; configKey is therefore null. step null: undocumented.

records.settingName: FOV (View Settings, per camera; plus FOV offset)

convention.basis: applies to the active render; independent of aspect ratio
convention.note: Converging specialist sources; not stated in current first-party documentation.

records.tripleNotes: ADJUDICATED CONFLICT: the 505 support article describing a single stretched span predates v1.8 and is HISTORICAL — current ACC has a distinct Triple Screen rendering mode (v1.8+ release notes reference bezel-line handling and DLSS/FSR in 'Triple Screen rendering mode'). Configure its physical geometry controls for triples. Pannini projection correction is available for non-triple wide rendering.
records.notes: The vertical convention is strong secondary consensus. Exact active-FOV JSON key is not defensibly verified (null). Community material names tripleWidth, tripleDistance, tripleAngle, and tripleBezel as JSON fields, but no direct supporting source was verified; those names are retained only as unverified research notes, not executable configuration guidance. step null: undocumented.

convention.basis: active projection; on native triples, the scalar setting represents the center-projection span
convention.note: OFFICIAL: Reiza staff state AMS2 uses horizontal FOV (not vertical like AMS1).

range.conditions: reported 16:9 single-screen baseline; range shifts with aspect ratio and triple state — not universal limits

records.menuPath: Options > Camera > Field of View ([ / ] keys live)
records.tripleNotes: Native triple configuration (angles/bezels computed by the game). Reiza guidance says to divide the calculated total triple horizontal span by 3 and enter that center span: 171° total -> 57; 178° total -> 59. Source: Reiza staff, 'Field of View - official statement' (official forum staff source below).
records.notes: The horizontal-axis claim remains high-confidence because it is a direct Reiza staff statement. Overall record confidence is medium because range, step, scalar-key behavior, and the triple center-span workflow are less comprehensively documented.

convention.basis: independent of aspect ratio; horizontal derived from aspect
convention.note: Encoded in the player.JSON key name 'Vertical FOV Angle'.

records.menuPath: Settings > Display > Vertical FOV (Ctrl+F cycles in-car; Ctrl+= opens triple monitor tool)
records.tripleNotes: MultiView renders one projection per screen with in-cockpit geometry editor. In multiview, configure physical geometry — do not enter the full envelope as one FOV. Single screen: map the calculated vertical span.
records.notes: One engine verified the player.JSON key from community config references; the other withheld it — retained WITH the caveat that it is not first-party documented. step null: undocumented.

convention.note: The setting is literally named 'Vertical FOV'.

range.conditions: default 49 (described as the developer's assumed-average-setup value)

records.tripleNotes: rF2-heritage multiview; users report enabling it locks/overrides the Vertical FOV scalar. Configure physical geometry in multiview; map the calculated vertical span on single screens. Per-screen angle/bezel UI extent not officially documented.
records.notes: Config key likely mirrors rF2's but was not verified for LMU — null beats a guess. step null: undocumented.

records.settingName: Field of View / Advanced FOV (physical-geometry calibration)

convention.note: UNRESOLVED: no current primary source names the scalar's axis. One research engine found community forum posts describing defaults of 58 vertical / 90 horizontal at 16:9; the other found secondary sources describing the basic scalar as horizontal. Prefer the Advanced FOV physical-geometry tool, which sidesteps the question.

records.units: degrees (current basic mode); physical measurements (Advanced FOV)
records.menuPath: Options > Field of View > Advanced FOV (Enter Calibration Mode: screen size + eye distance)
records.tripleNotes: Current RaceRoom supports true triple projection with bezel and angle correction (originally via -triScr launch argument, later in menus). Recommended path: use Advanced FOV with physical measurements. The legacy unitless fovScale multiplier (community mapping 1.0x≈58°) is OBSOLETE and deliberately not encoded.
records.notes: Both engines independently rated this the messiest record. Axis nulled rather than guessing between conflicting community claims; the tool should show the geometry-tool guidance instead of a numeric conversion.

records.settingName: Field of View (per camera mode; Driver camera)

convention.note: Engine documentation exposes vertical camera FOV (fovY); a developer forum reply says 'Vertical IIRC' (hedged); some community calculators claim horizontal — medium-low certainty.

range.conditions: verified for the core_camera.setFOV console path; UI slider range not separately verified

records.menuPath: Options > Camera > Camera: Driver > Field of View; triples: Options > Display > Multi-Monitor Render
records.tripleNotes: Official experimental Multi-Monitor Render: separate side projections, side-view angle sliders, requires 3 identical monitors + Borderless, Surround/Eyefinity off; NO bezel input documented. Without it, multi-monitor is one stretched span.
records.notes: Both engines converged on vertical via different evidence (engine fovY docs; hedged dev reply). Axis shown with its uncertainty in the UI. Vehicle JBeam camera sections may contain a per-vehicle fov default, but that is not verified as the user-facing Driver camera setting's executable config path/key; configFile and configKey are therefore null. step null: undocumented.

records.settingName: Field of View (Camera Customisation, per camera)

convention.note: Unitless offset around an undisclosed per-camera baseline.

records.menuPath: Settings > Camera Customisation > [camera] > Field of View
records.tripleNotes: No native monitor-angle, bezel, or separate-projection controls.
records.notes: EA publishes the offset range but not the baseline angle, axis, or projection equation. Community per-click estimates are reverse-engineered and deliberately excluded.

convention.note: Unitless 0-100 slider; EA's own forum reproductions dispute the rendered angles.

records.menuPath: Options & Extras > camera settings > Camera FOV
records.tripleNotes: No native multi-projection geometry controls verified.
records.notes: Per-camera community mappings exist but are reverse-engineered and disputed — no defensible conversion.

convention.note: Relative slider with no published unit mapping.

records.notes: The two research engines found DIFFERENT slider descriptions (0.0-1.0 display vs an 11-position relative slider from an archived developer quote whose original is unavailable) — range nulled; both agree the mapping to degrees is community reverse-engineering only.

convention.note: Values are degrees per Turn 10, but the axis and projection basis are undisclosed.

records.tripleNotes: Ultrawide/spanned rendering supported; no native angle/bezel/projection setup found.
records.notes: CORRECTED PREMISE: the game DOES have FOV sliders — the no-conversion verdict rests on the undisclosed axis/basis and the 65-degree cap, not absence.

records.menuPath: Quick Menu > Settings > Display Settings > Adjust Cockpit View (position only, not FOV)
records.tripleNotes: No native multi-monitor projection mode documented. Cockpit adjustments move the camera (height/depth/orientation/wobble) without changing the projection angle.
records.notes: Nothing to convert; community projection measurements deliberately not elevated into this dataset.
